content: I've been trying to cook more at home lately. Any tips for quick weeknight meals?
Absolutely! What kind of cuisine do you usually enjoy? That can help me suggest recipes that fit your taste.
I'm pretty adventurous with food. Last week I tried making a proper feijoada after visiting Brazil last year. Took me three days!
Wow, feijoada is quite the commitment! For weeknights, you might want to try one-pan dishes or sheet pan meals. They're much faster but still flavorful.
Sheet pan meals sound perfect. I'm usually exhausted after work - I'm a documentary filmmaker in Lisbon and my days can be really unpredictable.
Lisbon must be beautiful! For sheet pan meals, you can prep everything the night before. Have you tried Portuguese-style chicken with potatoes and peppers?
That sounds amazing, actually. I've been trying to cook more Portuguese dishes since moving here from Chicago five years ago. My parents are visiting next month and I want to impress them.
Chicago to Lisbon is quite the change! What do your parents like to eat? I can suggest some impressive but manageable Portuguese dishes for when they visit.
They're pretty traditional - my dad loves meat and potatoes, my mom is more adventurous but has trouble with spicy food now. I'm also trying to learn more about Portuguese wines to pair with meals.
For your dad, you could try bacalhau à brás - it's cod with potatoes and eggs, not spicy but very authentic. For wine, what's your budget? Portugal has great options at every price point.
I'd like to keep it under 15 euros a bottle if possible. I'm also thinking about making pastéis de nata for dessert - I've been practicing my baking skills during my parents' afternoon naps.
Pastéis de nata are perfect! For wines under 15 euros, look for Vinho Verde or a good Alentejo red. They pair wonderfully with cod dishes. How's your baking coming along?
Getting better! Though I burned the first batch - turns out my oven runs hot. I'm also trying to perfect my brigadeiros recipe as a nod to my Brazilian jiu-jitsu training group here in Lisbon.
